verbGlossary.BREAKS GROUND AT: {source} begins physical site work when the project reaches this open {target}, marking the start of construction activity.
verbGlossary.INSPECTED BEFORE BACKFILL VIA: {source} undergoes a mandatory inspection using {target} before being covered by subsequent work, ensuring hidden conditions are documented.
verbGlossary.PUMPS CONTINUOUSLY DURING: {source} operates without interruption while the project remains in this active {target}, maintaining dry working conditions.
verbGlossary.PASSES STORMWATER REVIEW AT: {source} satisfies regulatory compliance requirements and achieves this conforming {target} from the authority having jurisdiction.
verbGlossary.SUBMITS PILE DRIVING LOGS AT: {source} formally delivers installation records to the engineer of record when entering this submittal {target}.
verbGlossary.AWAITS LOAD TEST RESULTS DURING: {source} cannot proceed until geotechnical load test data is evaluated during this review {target}.
verbGlossary.STAGED FOR FOOTING INSPECTION AT: {source} is exposed and accessible, signaling readiness for the inspector to verify dimensions, rebar placement, and bearing conditions.
verbGlossary.FAILS FLATNESS TOLERANCE AT: {source} does not meet floor flatness (FF) or floor levelness (FL) specifications and receives this deficiency {target}.
verbGlossary.PROCEEDS TO BACKFILL UPON: {source} permits backfill operations to begin only after receiving {target}, protecting the membrane from premature loading.
verbGlossary.STOPS FOR CURE TIME AT: {source} pauses in this hold {target} while achieving required compressive strength before form stripping or loading.
verbGlossary.CLEARS PRESSURE TEST AT: {source} passes hydrostatic or air pressure testing and {target} closes, certifying the run is leak-free.
verbGlossary.PASSES CAMERA INSPECTION VIA: {source} undergoes CCTV camera inspection through this sequential {target} to verify slope, joints, and absence of debris.
verbGlossary.ADVANCES THROUGH UTILITY TIE-IN AT: {source} progresses through {target} when the connection to the municipal system is physically made and witnessed.
verbGlossary.POURS CONCRETE UPON: {source} receives its concrete placement only after formwork and reinforcement receive {target} from the structural engineer.
verbGlossary.RELEASES STEEL FABRICATION VIA: {source} shop drawings are approved through {target} pattern, authorizing the fabricator to begin production.
verbGlossary.CALLS FOR DECK INSPECTION AT: {source} notifies the inspector that formwork, rebar, and embeds are ready for verification before the concrete pour.
verbGlossary.COORDINATES EMBED PLACEMENT VIA: {source} is reviewed simultaneously by structural and MEP disciplines through this parallel review pattern to confirm no conflicts.
verbGlossary.TOPS OUT UPON: {source} structure achieves its final steel or concrete placement when {target} is granted, marking a major construction milestone.
verbGlossary.ROUTES SHOP DRAWINGS THROUGH: {source} fabrication drawings pass through this multi-party {target} chain including architect, structural engineer, and envelope consultant.
verbGlossary.RETURNS MOCKUP PANEL TO: {source} mockup fails performance testing (air, water, structural) and is returned to {target} for design revision and resubmission.
verbGlossary.ACCEPTS SUBSTITUTION AT: {source} is approved with noted deviations from the specification, permitting an alternate manufacturer or product to be installed.
verbGlossary.TARGETS DELIVERY WINDOW BY: {source} is tied to this due date, aligning factory shipment with the erection schedule on site.
verbGlossary.VERIFIED BEFORE CLADDING VIA: {source}is inspected through {target} before exterior cladding is installed, since the membrane will be permanently concealed.
verbGlossary.PASSES FLOOD TEST AT: {source} achieves this conforming {target} after standing water is held on the surface for 48 hours with no leaks detected.
verbGlossary.FLAGGED FOR IMPROPER LAP AT: {source} fails inspection because overlaps do not meet minimum requirements, receiving this deficient {target} requiring corrective work.
verbGlossary.DELAYS ENCLOSURE MILESTONE AT: {source} has missed its procurement or installation deadline, pushing the building enclosure milestone into this overdue {target}.
verbGlossary.RESOLVES COORDINATION CLASH VIA: {source} participates in this review-and-respond {target} to resolve spatial conflicts identified in the coordination model with other MEP trades.
verbGlossary.QUEUED FOR STARTUP COMMISSIONING AT: {source} equipment is installed and awaiting the commissioning agent to perform startup testing at this {target}.
verbGlossary.MEASURES POINT-TO-POINT CHECKOUT BY: {source} tracks completion of individual control point verification using {target} completion rate metric.
verbGlossary.COMPLETES BALANCING REPORT AT: {source}testing, adjusting, and balancing work finishes its final report and {target} reaches this closed status, certifying airflows and water flows meet design.
verbGlossary.ROUTES HYDRAULIC CALCULATIONS THROUGH: {source} main's hydraulic calculations pass through sequential review by the fire protection engineer and authority having jurisdiction.
verbGlossary.EXEMPTS UNOCCUPIED ZONES AT: {source} is marked as not applicable in areas that do not require coverage per code, such as certain utility rooms.
verbGlossary.LOGS ACCEPTANCE TEST VIA: {source} records its final acceptance test results through this create-and-close {target}, documenting every device and circuit.
verbGlossary.ENERGIZES SWITCHGEAR VIA: {source}receives authorization to be energized through {target}, requiring the responsible electrician's formal sign-off.
verbGlossary.ASSIGNS SLEEVE LAYOUT OWNER VIA: {source} sleeve and penetration coordination is routed to a responsible party through {target}.
verbGlossary.MEASURES FIXTURE APPROVAL RATE BY: {source} submittal success rate is tracked using this first-pass {target} metric to gauge specification compliance across fixtures.
verbGlossary.CALLS FOR ABOVE-CEILING INSPECTION AT: {source}als readiness for inspection of all MEP rough-ins above the ceiling plane before the ceiling is closed.
verbGlossary.COATS SEQUENTIALLY DURING: {source} applies primer, intermediate, and finish coats in sequence while the work remains in this active progress {target}.
verbGlossary.FAILS LIPPAGE STANDARD AT: {source} does not meet allowable height variation between adjacent tiles and receives this rejection requiring re-installation.
verbGlossary.TRACKS SHOP DRAWING TURNAROUND BY: {source} monitors how quickly the architect reviews and returns shop drawings using this response time metric.
verbGlossary.ADVANCES THROUGH ACCEPTANCE TESTING VIA: {source} car progresses through this multi-stage financial review pattern encompassing safety tests, load tests, and final code inspection.
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS REINSPECTION NOTICE AT: {source} machinery fails initial inspection and this escalation {target} notifies all parties that a reinspection is required.
verbGlossary.MONITORS WEATHER DELAY BACKLOG BY: {source} tracks the cumulative impact of weather-related schedule delays using this overdue rate metric.
verbGlossary.ACHIEVES SUBSTANTIAL COMPLETION AT: {source} installation marks the final scope item and the project {target} reaches this closed status.
verbGlossary.MEASURES INTEGRATION TESTING LAG BY: {source} tracks how long individual control sequences remain in testing {target} using this dwell time metric.
